preamble: Debuccalization of [g] to the glottal fricative [h] is a common form of lenition, notably found in Galician (Gheada) and Southern Dutch dialects.

examples.note: West and East Slavic *g → voiced glottal fricative [ɦ] (not [h]). E.g., Czech 'hora' (mountain) vs. Russian 'gora' (both from PS *gora). Ukrainian also shows this shift.

sources: Sussex, R. & Cubberley, P. (2006). The Slavic Languages. Cambridge University Press.
